verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is a secreted cytokine synthesized as a preproprotein and processed into a latent complex (mature TGFB1 homodimer + LAP + LTBP) or an active form. The latent complex is sequestered in the extracellular matrix and becomes surface-accessible only upon context-dependent activation (mechanical tension, proteolytic cleavage, integrin-mediated release, acidic microenvironment, reactive oxygen species, thrombospondin binding). Soluble active TGFB1 can transiently recruit to cell-surface TGF-beta receptors but does not carry its own membrane anchor. The latent form's association with LTBP and ECM deposition qualifies as stable extracellular tethering in tissue context, and integrin-mediated release from ECM onto cells is a major biological mechanism. This positions TGFB1 as contextually surface-accessible via activation and ECM sequestration rather than constitutively or via its own TM/GPI mechanism.
